metadata.title: Leviathan Australian Intrusions Water and Wastewater Attack Scenario

Scenario.overview: Water and Wastewater Systems Simple This attack scenario involves basic exploitation techniques targeting water and wastewater systems in Australia.
Scenario.attack_surface: AVAILABLE_SURFACES: Internet-facing assets, Active Directory, Scheduled Task, Network Traffic, File
Scenario.attack_vector: AVAILABLE_VECTORS: General Access Method, Custom Command and Control Protocol, Scheduled Task/Job, Network Traffic Content (Mechanism), File Modification (Mechanism)

stage.name: Initial Access
stage.description: The attacker exploits public-facing applications with known vulnerabilities to gain initial access.

stage.name: Initial Access
stage.description: The attacker compromises websites visited by employees, infecting them with malware through drive-by downloads.

stage.name: Execution
stage.technique_name: T1094: Custom Command and Control Protocol
stage.description: The attacker uses custom command and control protocols to communicate with compromised devices and execute malicious commands.

stage.name: Execution
stage.description: The attacker creates scheduled tasks to run malicious code at specific times, maintaining persistence and executing malicious actions.

stage.name: Impact
stage.description: The attacker disrupts network traffic, disabling critical system resources and preventing them from functioning as intended.

stage.name: Impact
stage.capec: CAPEC-675: Retrieve Data from Decommissioned Devices
stage.description: The attacker deletes critical data stored in files, causing significant financial losses and damage to infrastructure.

Scenario.summary: This attack scenario demonstrates how basic exploitation techniques can be used to compromise water and wastewater systems in Australia, resulting in significant disruptions and financial losses.
